head: e6d31b61

vs_past.note: B47 W7 flash-lite scored 0V/5I/2R vs B46 5V/2I/0R. Regression on rubric scenarios (S1, S5). No rubric on S2/S3/S4/S6/S7 — those are inconclusive by design.

pr290_observation: 1 invoke_skill_spawn_ack_exit event observed (S6, index_docs skill spawn). 0 literal-echo strings found across all 33 turns ('is running in the background' / 'バックグラウンドで実行' / 'Use `/tasks` to monitor').

scenarios.reason: Rubric requires final turn addresses P7/skill-specific leakage prevention and references prior context. Turn 5 reply: 'I cannot answer your question because I lack information about the OS in Reyn's architecture.' — fails both rubric items. Turn 2 also failed to find skill docs. Empty-stop retry fired 1 time.

rubric_items.item: final answer addresses skill-specific leakage prevention (P7 or equivalent)
references concepts established in earlier turns without re-explaining from scratch
does not produce an empty reply on the final turn

scenarios.reason: No rubric/expected block. All 6 turns produced non-empty replies. Pronoun chain partially maintained (turn 2 gave info about simplest skill; turns 3-6 answered contextually). 3 empty-stop retries fired, all resolved via REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY. No rubric to evaluate pass/fail definitively.

No rubric/expected block. All 5 turns completed. Cross-reference maintained: turn 5 references both LangGraph (graph-based) and Reyn (peer-to-peer) from earlier turns. Turn 4 noted async comparison inconclusive due to tool limits. Substantive answers throughout. 5 empty-stop retries fired.

No rubric/expected block. All 6 repetitive turns completed with substantive skill-list answers. No empty-stop retries fired (0) — suggesting context-bloat did NOT trigger G12-type empty completions on this scenario. This is a positive signal for the REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY fix.

Rubric requires final turn produce a working minimal async producer-consumer queue using asyncio.Queue. Turn 5 reply: 'I am unable to provide a minimal example of an async producer-consumer queue at this time.' The agent searched for tools/actions instead of reasoning directly. Fails all rubric items. 2 empty-stop retries fired.

rubric_items.item: final reply contains a working minimal async producer-consumer queue example in Python
the code uses asyncio.Queue or equivalent
does not produce an empty reply on the final turn

scenarios.reason: No rubric. Skill run spawned (index_docs) but failed with 'allow-unsafe-python not provided' — env reason, qualifying as inconclusive per verdict rules. Tool 'source__glob' also not in catalog. 1 invoke_skill_spawn_ack_exit event observed (spawn_ack_count=1). 3 empty-stop retries fired.
scenarios.skill_run_failed_reason: Skill 'index_docs' declares an unsafe python step (./chunkers.py:gather_samples) but --allow-unsafe-python was not provided.

scenarios.reason: No rubric. All 5 turns on distributed systems (eventual consistency, CAP theorem, concrete examples, runtime detection, repair strategies) produced substantive answers with good context continuity. Turn 4 and 5 generated 6043 and 5558 chars respectively — longest replies in W7 batch. 1 empty-stop retry fired.

new_findings.title: flash-lite falls back to tool-search instead of direct reasoning for general knowledge
new_findings.description: S5 (Python asyncio) and S1 (Reyn architecture) both show flash-lite attempting to find 'actions' or tool results for factual questions it should answer directly. When tool search fails, it refuses to answer. This is a tool-bias attractor: the model treats every question as requiring a tool call. S5 turn 5 explicitly searched 'asyncio producer consumer queue example' via tool, got no results, then refused to generate code. Rubric items requiring direct reasoning output both failed.
new_findings.hypothesis: flash-lite has stronger tool-call affordance bias than expected — prefers tool search over direct LLM reasoning even for general knowledge questions.

new_findings.title: REYN_EMPTY_STOP_RETRY=1 active across all scenarios; S4 (repetitive context) fired 0 retries
new_findings.description: Empty-stop retries fired in S1(1), S2(3), S3(5), S4(0), S5(2), S6(3), S7(1). Notably S4 (the primary G12 attractor scenario: repetitive context-bloat) fired 0 retries, suggesting the repeated context pattern did not trigger empty completions under flash-lite in this run. S3 fired the most (5 retries across 5 turns = 1 per turn), suggesting cross-reference scenario is highest empty-stop risk.
new_findings.hypothesis: G12 empty-stop attractor may be model-tier dependent; flash-lite's lower capability may produce non-empty but low-quality refusals rather than empty stops.

new_findings.title: PR #290 spawn_ack accumulation: 1 event observed in W7 (S6 only)
new_findings.description: 1 invoke_skill_spawn_ack_exit event with spawn_ack_count=1 observed in S6 when index_docs skill was spawned. Literal-echo count = 0 across all 33 turns — REYN_SPAWN_ACK_TO_LLM=1 did not cause echo text to appear in replies. S6 skill failed before ACK could be meaningfully delivered (allow-unsafe-python not provided), so full ACK-to-LLM flow was not tested end-to-end.
